lanes.mission: Turn Close context into the next best reply or follow-up move.
lanes.done: Andre can see who needs contact now and what the next message should aim to do.
lanes.whatWasDone: CRM priorities already drive Mission Control and now have named ownership.
lanes.pending: Keep message-quality signals tied to the lane over time.
lanes.blocked: No explicit message-quality indicator is shown yet.
lanes.nextAction: Keep the hottest reply and follow-up work ahead of side tasks.

lanes.mission: Push qualified leads toward calls, tastings, and booked events.
lanes.done: The highest-leverage conversion moves are visible before generic nurture work.
lanes.whatWasDone: Conversion ownership is now explicit for tasting-stage work.
lanes.pending: Add stronger conversion summary signals later.
lanes.blocked: No dedicated conversion scorecard exists yet.
lanes.nextAction: Keep booked and setting-tasting leads at the top of the operator focus list.

lanes.mission: Keep intake and automation handoffs clean, canonical, and useful.
lanes.done: Leads enter the right path without confusion or hidden dependencies.
lanes.whatWasDone: Lane ownership is defined and ready for canonical inbox cleanup.
lanes.pending: Align intake around the canonical team inbox path.
lanes.blocked: Public form and legacy Gmail path are still mismatched.
lanes.nextAction: Run the intake alignment pass and mark legacy pieces clearly.

lanes.mission: Keep Mission Control premium, readable, and fast to scan.
lanes.done: Andre can glance at the page and know who owns what and what is next.
lanes.whatWasDone: A compact squad accountability surface is being added to Mission Control.
lanes.pending: Tune spacing and hierarchy after the first pass is visible.
lanes.nextAction: Render the lane ownership table without cluttering the dashboard.

lanes.mission: Keep the repo, data build, and Close guardrail reliable.
lanes.done: Mission Control refreshes cleanly and the codebase stays trustworthy.
lanes.whatWasDone: Data build path reviewed and extended for squad accountability.
lanes.pending: Keep the accountability payload easy to maintain.
lanes.nextAction: Regenerate latest.json with the new squad payload and verify rendering.

lanes.mission: Decide what is worth building, automating, or paying attention to.
lanes.done: The system emphasizes high-value work and starves low-value noise.
lanes.whatWasDone: ROI and prioritization now have a named lane instead of being implicit.
lanes.pending: Add stronger ROI framing to project backlog later.
lanes.blocked: Current project list does not yet show explicit ROI ranking.
lanes.nextAction: Keep dashboard additions small and operator-useful.

lanes.mission: Use media only when it supports sales or brand trust.
lanes.done: Media work directly supports a live sales or brand objective.
lanes.whatWasDone: Lane defined so media work stays optional, not distracting.
lanes.blocked: No live Comeketo media objective is surfaced right now.
lanes.nextAction: Stay quiet until a real sales-supporting media need appears.

lanes.mission: Preserve rules, continuity, and operational clarity.
lanes.done: The system can be explained simply and run without drift.
lanes.whatWasDone: Role map, lane map, and status format have been formalized.
lanes.pending: Keep the status surface current instead of letting it decay.
lanes.nextAction: Use the shared reporting format across future lane updates.

lanes.mission: Decide what happens next across the whole operating system.
lanes.done: Mission Control answers what Andre should do next and why.
lanes.whatWasDone: The squad now has a visible accountability layer tied back to Mission Control.
lanes.pending: Keep lane status current as live work changes.
lanes.nextAction: Use CRM and conversion pressure to drive the day before side projects.
